name: Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail
name_original: Punta Ballota y Ensenada de Calderón – circular desde Tagle
description: Hike the moderate 9.6-mile Punta Ballota and Ensenada de Calderón circular trail from Tagle for stunning Cantabrian coastal views.
region_intro: Hiking around Suances offers diverse landscapes along the Cantabrian coastline in northern Spain. The region features dramatic cliffs, lush green hills, and pristine beaches. It is characterized by the confluence of the Saja and Besaya rivers, forming the San Martín de la Arena estuary. Hikers can experience coastal paths, riverine trails, and viewpoints offering panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay. Best hiking trails around Suances The most popular hiking route is Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail, a 9.6 miles (15.5 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This route provides extensive views of the dramatic Cantabrian coastline. Another top favourite among local hikers is Playa de Los Locos – View from Suances Lighthouse loop from Suances, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail offers views of the famous surfing beach and the Suances Lighthouse perched on a cliff. Local hikers also love the Suances – Los Cantos Pier loop from Suances, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the town, often completed in about 2 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Suances is defined by dramatic cliffs, pristine beaches, and lush green hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Suances are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Suances's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Suances?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Suances, offering a variety of experiences. These include 10 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and a few more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Suances?
A: Yes, Suances offers 10 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10507572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Los Locos – View from Suances Lighthouse loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.6-mile (5.8 km) path that provides scenic views of the famous surfing beach and the lighthouse.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Suances?
A: Hiking in Suances offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter dramatic cliffs, lush green hills, and pristine beaches along the Cantabrian coastline. The area is also shaped by the San Martín de la Arena estuary, where the Saja and Besaya rivers meet, providing varied coastal and riverine scenery.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Suances?
A: Yes, many routes in Suances are designed as circular walks, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24651300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suances – Los Cantos Pier loop from Suances&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.1-mile (9.9 km) trail that takes you through coastal areas and the town.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Suances is rich in scenic viewpoints and landmarks. You can enjoy spectacular views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1511383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Ballota&lt;/a&gt;, explore the historic ruins of Torco de Afuera, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint. The Suances Lighthouse also offers stunning panoramic vistas of the coastline.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Suances?
A: Many of the easier and shorter trails in Suances are suitable for families. These routes often lead to beautiful beaches or offer accessible viewpoints, providing enjoyable outings for all ages. Consider trails that explore the beaches or the estuary for a pleasant family experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Suances?
A: The best time to hike in Suances is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it can be warmer and more crowded, especially along the coast.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Suances?
A: The hiking trails in Suances are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic coastal views, the variety of landscapes from cliffs to beaches, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, Suances offers more challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998500848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagle Cliffs Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.6-mile (15.5 km) route that provides extensive views of the dramatic Cantabrian coastline and requires a good level of fitness.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to beaches or offer coastal access?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Suances are coastal, leading directly to or offering stunning views of the area's beautiful beaches, such as Los Locos Beach or Playa El Sable. These routes often feature dramatic cliff backdrops and opportunities for refreshing seaside walks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible via hiking trails?
A: While hiking, you can discover several historical and cultural sites. The ruins of Torco de Afuera, an 18th-century fortification, are accessible via trails. Additionally, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt; in Santillana del Mar is a significant historical landmark that can be combined with a hike in the broader region.
tips: Be careful approaching the cliffs on the right side. Vegetation covers the edge.
El Sable Beach, located in Tagle, a town in the municipality of Suances (Cantabria), is a natural gem perfect for those seeking tranquility and scenic beauty.
Beautiful hermitage literally embedded in the rock, and in the middle of a cove worth visiting.

Beautiful views of the Hermitage of Santa Justa that is literally embedded in a cliff, which makes it an incredibly beautiful place. Seeing how the waves break practically next to the hermitage is wonderful. There is a very small beach at the foot of the hermitage, and right next to the car park there is a beach bar and a picnic area.
A wonderful hideaway to take a bath, if the weather was good, which was not the case.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts near the small 18th-century hermitage of Guadalupe in Tagle. Parking is generally available in the village of Tagle, which serves as the starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: How difficult is this hike?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a moderate hike. It requires a good level of fitness due to its length and some challenging descents, particularly to Ensenada de Calderón. Sure-footedness is recommended, especially near clifftops.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 15.5 kilometers (9.6 miles) long and typically takes around 4 hours and 20 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter roads (initially asphalt), wider tracks, and smaller paths that follow the clifftops. Some descents can be challenging, but many sections offer comfortable walking.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning coastal views and natural beauty. Highlights include panoramic vistas from Punta Ballota, the natural beauty of El Sable Beach, and the unique Ermita de Santa Justa built into a cliff. You'll also pass Santa Justa Beach, Onzapera Cove, and Calderón Cove, with remnants of old mining activity at Punta del Poyo.

faqs.question: Is the Punta Ballota and Ensenada de Calderón circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially near cliffs and in areas where livestock might be present. Please follow local regulations regarding leashes and waste disposal.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this coastal trail in Cantabria is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. The weather is usually milder and more pleasant for hiking, avoiding the peak summer heat and larger crowds.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other official routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section of this route (approximately 3%) follows the official trail known as Calle El Coterón.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Tagle is a small village, and you may find some local establishments. For more options, the nearby town of Suances offers a wider selection of cafes and restaurants.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and coastal exposure, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. A camera is also recommended for the stunning views.

waypoints.name: View of the Hermitage of Santa Justa
